page: Amazon Web Services c8ib.large is a compute-optimized instance belonging to the c8ib family, powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor operating at 3.9 GHz. This configuration provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. The processor features a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations.

The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is delivered via a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities, with gzip compression reaching 33.81 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.37 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are generally limited. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is average at 2110.08, while the multi-core performance is poor at 2153.47, reflecting the single physical core limitation. Memory bandwidth tests show weak results, with read-only large block speeds at 23.9 GB/sec and write speeds at 15.85 GB/sec.

Given its hardware profile, the c8ib.large offers a specialized entry-level compute option. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that benefit from high clock speeds and advanced Intel instruction sets (such as AMX or AVX-512) but do not require massive parallelization or high memory capacity. Suitable workloads include lightweight single-threaded processing, basic web serving, and small-scale vector or matrix calculations.
description: Amazon Web Services c8ib.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 2 vCPUs from a single physical core and is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While single-threaded performance is strong, as demonstrated by high gzip and bzip3 speeds, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are relatively weak due to the single-core hardware constraint. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. It is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded compute tasks, basic web serving, and workloads utilizing specialized Intel instruction sets.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8ib.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 2 vCPUs, and 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8ib.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A dedicated single-core Intel Xeon instance with DDR5 memory and advanced matrix extensions for lightweight compute workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
4.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz
Support for Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets
Strong single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic web serving
